data.title: Efficient Grammar Compression via RLZ-based RePair
data.authors: Rahul Varki, Travis Gagie, Christina Boucher
data.description: Among grammar-based compression techniques, RePair is a notable offline encoding scheme known for its simplicity and powerful combinatorial properties, producing compact grammars by repeatedly replacing the most frequent adjacent pairs of symbols, known as bigrams. However, RePair’s memory usage scales poorly with input size, as it loads the entire text into memory. In contrast, Relative Lempel-Ziv (RLZ) parsing offers a scalable and lightweight online encoding scheme that losslessly represents a text in terms of phrases that refer to a reference string, but it often fails to expose deeper structural patterns. We introduce an algorithm that produces a RePair grammar from the RLZ parse of the input, leveraging the strengths of both methods. Our method, RLZ-RePair, performs bigram replacements systematically, preserving the integrity of the RLZ phrases throughout the RePair iterations. When the reference …
